place: Cape Yamu, Pa Klok, Thalang, Phuket, Thailand
title: Villa Amaravida — Scandinavian-Thai Estate
blurb: The villa from HBO's The White Lotus — an eight-bedroom Cape Yamu estate of Scandinavian-Thai design over 4,000 m², with three infinity pools, a spa and sauna, cinema, wine cellar, and private tennis, basketball and squash courts.
detail:
An absolute prime among the villas on Phuket's Cape Yamu, Villa Amaravida exudes elegance and tropical resplendence overlooking Phang Nga Bay. Its motif marries Scandinavian architecture with traditional Thai roots — a Thai-gabled roof outside, clean minimalist lines, marble and wood within. Set on 8,000 m² of land with a 4,000 m² build, the estate carries a long list of activities for a grand villa experience, and is known worldwide as a filming location for HBO's The White Lotus.

Indulge in three pools — a 38-metre main infinity pool, a mid-level pool and a lap pool — plus two large jacuzzis, one on a rooftop bar that seats up to 16. Eight en-suite bedrooms include a master suite on its own floor, all with uninterrupted seascapes. Sports and fitness are covered by a full gym, private tennis, basketball and squash courts, and a playground with climbing wall, while a man-made beach, kayaks and paddleboards open up the water.

Entertainment runs deep: a cinema room with a 12-seat couch, a game room with Trackman golf simulator, a large wine cellar, and a spa room with steam and sauna for winding down. Served by seven staff including a private chef, with a fully equipped American kitchen and estate restaurants nearby, Amaravida sits in a gated community with 24-hour security, 21 km from the airport.
